title: Batch dynamically with a bounded queue and timeout
description: Coalesce concurrent requests into batches up to a max size or max wait, so the accelerator stays busy without starving latency.
body: Use when serving a model on a GPU/accelerator that is more efficient per-batch than per-request. Do not serve one request at a time (the GPU idles between calls) and do not batch unboundedly (latency and memory blow up). Actions: set a max batch size and a max queue wait (e.g. a few milliseconds), form a batch when either limit is hit, and tune both against the latency SLO. Validation signals include high GPU utilization with p99 still under SLO and a queue that drains. Risk boundary: an unbounded batch window trades tail latency for throughput invisibly; if p99 regresses, cap the wait and escalate before raising it.
